ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR First Amended Petition For Instructions Etc.
Off Calendar Dec-10-2025 Continued To Apr-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Edward Judge for Feliciana Medina, Moving Party (via Zoom).
Aya Kanda for Kisha Palmer as an individual. (via Zoom).
Justin Hedemark for Kisha Palmer in her capacity as trustee (via Zoom).

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 20, 2026. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 30, 2026. The matter is continued to April 6, 2026 and the Parties are to return on April 6, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

The Court shall issue a Breslin order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

James Cummins and Aya Kanda for Kisha Palmer as an individual. (via Zoom).
Justin Hedemark for Kisha Palmer in her capacity as trustee (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding mediation, that parties are all interested in mediation, and that parties need to work on some of their differences and have access to Ms. Fontes medical records. The matter is continued to December 10, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Belen. Not reported.

Motion For Relief From Courts February 20, 2025 Order; And For Leave To File Objection To First Amended Petition
Off Calendar Jul-23-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Edward Judge and Rafael Godoy for Feliciana Medina, Moving Party (via Zoom).
Jim Cummins and Aya Kanda for Kisha Palmer as an individual.
Justin Hedemark for Kisha Palmer in her capacity as trustee (via Zoom).

The Court called the matter on Petitioner Feliciano Medina's motion to vacate the Court's February 20, 2025 order. Mr. Judge challenged the Court's tentative ruling to deny the motion and requested that the Court exercise its equitable powers under Code of Civil Procedure Section 473(b) to set aside the order.

Mr. Judge contended that Ms. Medina's failure to appear on February 18, 2025, was the result of excusable neglect and emphasized her intent to object to the first amended petition had she attended. He asserted that the case involved colorable claims regarding disputed interlineations in the trust instrument and argued that Ms. Medina was not adequately informed of the consequences of the hearing. He further argued that the trustee had a duty of impartiality and should not oppose the motion to vacate.

Mr. Hedemark clarified that the trustee had filed a response to provide procedural context and not to advocate for either beneficiary. He confirmed that all trust assets had been liquidated and were held in a single account totaling approximately $450,000, reduced due to a reverse mortgage. He stated the trustee had advised both parties to seek independent counsel.

Mr. Cummins argued in opposition to the motion, asserting that Ms. Medina failed to demonstrate a sufficient basis under 473(b), and that her mistake was legal in nature-not a calendaring error. He emphasized that Ms. Medina had received communications from the trustee's counsel regarding the disputed trust amendments and failed to take appropriate action over a prolonged period.

Mr. Judge responded that the first amended petition served as advocacy for one beneficiary over another, and that Ms. Medina, as a self-represented and unsophisticated party, was denied a fair opportunity to present objections. He urged the Court to vacate the order in the interest of equity and due process.

After hearing oral argument, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Shivanee Sujata Singh, CSR #13983, depos@keltyscott.com, 650-366-3008 (D204)

Petition For Instructions And Order 1. Determining Effect Of Interlineations 2. Determining Current Vested Beneficiaries 3. Approving And Authorizing
Off Calendar Feb-19-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Justin Hedemark for Kisha Palmer, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The court acknowledged the petitioner's residence in Seattle and the potential venue issue. The court decided to waive the venue objection and accept jurisdiction in San Francisco under CCP 392(a)(1), citing the petition's request for an order to determine the trust's current vested beneficiaries. Mr. Hedemark confirmed that no objections or communications from any interested parties had been received as of the hearing date.

The court accepted the finding that the settlor's handwritten interlineations made in 2018 and 2019 were valid amendments to the trust. The court took the petition under submission with the intent to grant the petition as prayed.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
